name: Memory Architecture Idioms
description: Definition and usage patterns for the Antigravity 8-Tier Memory System.

patterns.semantic: Verified, long-term technical knowledge (KIs, Rules, Axioms). Use for grounding strategy.
patterns.procedural: Step-by-step SOPs (Workflows). Use for operational guidance.
patterns.toolbox: Tactical capabilities (Skills, Blueprints, Templates, MCP configs). Use to find the right tool for a specific job.
patterns.entity: System components, Personas, and relational nodes. Use for context enrichment.
patterns.summary: Distilled session logs and high-level history. Use to maintain context without bloat.
patterns.episodic: Raw session observations and raw logs. Use for immediate short-term memory and history retrieval.
patterns.pending: HITL Induction Queue. Memory candidates awaiting verification. Use for the learning loop.
patterns.rejected: Blacklisted insights or failed proposals. Use to prevent repetitive errors.

best_practices: Always separate production memory from test memory using RAG_COLLECTION_PREFIX.
Index early, index often to keep the RAG synchronized with filesystem assets.
Use the Induction Queue (Pending) for any non-verified knowledge before promotion to Semantic.
anti_patterns: Directly writing to Semantic memory without a verification step.
Wiping the Qdrant localhost:6333 without checking worker isolation prefixes.
Leaving the Toolbox empty (it should always map to the current Skill/Blueprint library).
